articles.title: SMR Jewels IPO Day 4: Issue remains undersubscribed so far. Here's GMP, and other details
articles.summary: According to platforms tracking the unofficial market, the grey market premium (GMP) for the SMR Jewels IPO is currently nil. Based on the current GMP, the stock is expected to list at same as the issue price.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Do you know what ADRs are? A complete guide for investors
articles.summary: American Depositary Receipts enable US investors to easily buy shares of foreign companies. While once popular among Indian investors, newer investment avenues have reduced ADRs' significance, which now cater to specialised needs such as tracking price differences and specific asset allocations.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Zee Entertainment share price zooms 7% as firm to broadcast 2026 football world cup in India
articles.summary: Shares of Zee Entertainment Limited (ZEEL) surged 7% in intraday deals on Monday, 1 June, after the company announced partnering with FIFA to broadcast the 2026 football world cup in India.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rajnandini Fashion IPO allotment date likely today. GMP, steps to check share allotment status online for SME IPO
articles.summary: Rajnandini Fashion IPO allotment date is likely today, June 1. Investors can check Rajnandini Fashion IPO allotment status online through the websites of BSE and the IPO registrar. Bigshare Services is the Rajnandini Fashion India IPO registrar.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Hexagon Nutrition IPO: From financials to key risks — 10 key things to know from the RHP ahead of offer launch
articles.summary: Hexagon Nutrition IPO would be the first mainboard offering to hit Dalal Street in a month. So far this year, only 20 IPOs have opened for bidding this year with the market sentiment impacted by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Aureate Tradde IPO Day 2: Check GMP, subscription status, and other key details
articles.summary: The shares of Aureate Tradde IPO are commanding a premium of ₹13 in the grey market, as per websites tracking the unlisted market.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Data centres are powering Cummins. Can growth justify the valuation?
articles.summary: Strong demand from hyperscalers and colocation operators is driving growth in Cummins India’s power-generation business, though a rich valuation and export headwinds could cap further upside.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: AMFI stock re-categorisation: BSE, Vodafone Idea, Jindal Steel, BHEL among potential large-cap entrants
articles.summary: AMFI stock re-categorisation: Stocks that could potentially move into the large-cap basket include BSE, Jindal Steel & Power, Vodafone Idea, Hitachi Energy India, Indian Bank, Indus Towers and Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd (BHEL).
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rupee depreciation blunts India bond's appeal for foreign investors
articles.summary: Rupee depreciating roughly 3.4% against the dollar since the beginning of the West Asia war, made Indian bonds unattractive.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Suzlon Energy share price extends losses for second straight session. Details here
articles.summary: Suzlon Energy share price dropped nearly 5% after SEBI imposed penalties of around ₹29 crore for financial reporting violations. The regulator found multiple infractions by the company and former executives, stemming from a 2019 anonymous tip that initiated a detailed investigation.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: HDFC Bank share price trades in green after losing for three straight sessions
articles.summary: HDFC Bank share price trend has largely remained negative amid weak market sentiment. The private lender stock has fallen by 5.20% in a week and 3.34% in a month.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Acme Solar share price jumps to all-time high amid reports of ₹2,500 crore QIP move
articles.summary: Acme Solar share price has gained nearly 4% in one month and surged more than 33% in three months. The stock has rallied 39% in six months and has risen 19% in one year.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Inox Wind share price slumps over 8% after Q4 results. Should you buy, sell or hold?
articles.summary: Inox Wind share price fell over 8% on June 1 after a 45% drop in Q4 FY26 net profit to ₹105.68 crore, attributed to rising operating expenses. Despite this, the company's order backlog reached 3.1 GW, ensuring revenue stability for over two years.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Indian stock market: Nifty 50 hovers around key support of 23,500. What should investors do?
articles.summary: On June 1, the Nifty 50 traded at 23,516.50, down 0.13%, amid investor caution and foreign fund outflows. Support is seen at 23,500, with market analysts suggesting that maintaining this level is crucial for stability as attention shifts to geopolitical factors and upcoming monetary policy.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: 800% returns in five years! Multibagger small-cap stock jumps 7%. Here's why
articles.summary: Rathi Steel and Power stock opened at ₹20.63 apiece on 1 June, as compared to previous close of ₹18.76 on Friday last week.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Weak debut! M.R.Maniveni Foods share price lists at 18% discount to IPO price on BSE SME
articles.summary: M.R.Maniveni Foods share price listed at ₹42.55, down ₹9.45 or 18.17% compared with the offer price of ₹52. Soon after listing, however, the SME stock recovered a bit. It touched the day's peak of ₹44.66, rising almost 5% from the listing price but remained below its offer price.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Merritronix IPO fully subscribed on first bidding day. Retail, NIIs steal the show; Check GMP, issue details
articles.summary: Merritronix IPO commenced on June 1 and concludes on June 3, with a price range of ₹141-149 per share. The company raised ₹19.91 crore from anchor investors and has seen strong subscription demand, with a current GMP of +88, indicating a promising market debut.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: MCX launches ‘Silver 100’ futures from June 1, offering smaller-ticket silver exposure: All you need to know
articles.summary: Silver 100 contracts will be compulsorily settled through physical delivery, with Ahmedabad designated as the delivery centre through MCX Clearing Corporation-accredited facilities. The delivery unit has been fixed at 100 grams.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Concor fails to impress in Q4, but the Street isn't giving up just yet
articles.summary: Falling market share at Mundra, and weak rail modal share remain key concerns for Concor investors, though gains at Jawaharlal Nehru Port (JNPT) have offered some relief.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: JP Power, Vodafone Idea, Suzlon, Ola Electric among most traded stocks on NSE today, 1 June 2026
articles.summary: JP Power, Vodafone Idea, NMDC Steel, Suzlon Energy, YES Bank, Ola Electric Mobility, Dharan Infra-EPC, PC Jeweller, and GTL Infrastructure were the most traded stocks on the NSE.

articles.title: Adani Power, Adani Ports to Adani Total Gas: Adani group stocks tank up to 4%. Details here

articles.title: Mukul Agrawal-owned PTC Industries surges 19.5% after Q4 results. Do you own this multibagger stock?
articles.summary: PTC Industries share price rose as much as 19.51% against its last closing price and inched nearer to its 52-week high after the earnings announcement for the March quarter.

articles.title: How Charlie Munger found an ideal investment option during the global financial crisis in 2008?
articles.summary: What began as a contrarian investment during one of the worst financial crises eventually turned into a multi-billion-dollar success story, with Berkshire reportedly earning profits of around $10 billion by the time it substantially exited the investment in 2025.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Dharmesh Shah of ICICI Sec suggests buying Tata Power, Sona BLW shares on 1 June
articles.summary: Indian benchmark indices opened higher on June 1, supported by gains in IndiGo and Asian Paints. The NSE Nifty rose 0.38% and the BSE Sensex gained 0.36% amid positive Asian market sentiment and recovery after recent sell-offs.

articles.title: Crude oil prices rise 2% as concerns about Middle East conflict linger; Brent at $93. What's the near-term outlook?
articles.summary: US-Iran war: MCX crude oil prices rose as much as 3.15% to ₹8,542 per barrel. Here's what analysts say on the near-term outlook.

articles.title: Reliance Industries share price jumps on Gujarat lignite gasification project with NLC India
articles.summary: Reliance Industries shares rose over 1% after announcing a partnership with NLC India to explore an underground lignite gasification project in Gujarat, aiming to enhance energy security and reduce dependence on LNG.

articles.title: Vardhman Textiles, Gokaldas Exports to KPR Mill: Textile stocks jump up to 6% after cotton import duty exemption
articles.summary: Vardhman Textiles share price jumped over 6%, followed by Pearl Global Industries, Raymond Lifestyle and Trident shares rallying over 5%. Gokaldas Exports, KPR Mill and Welspun Living shares gained over 4% each, while Alok Industries share price surged over 3%.

articles.title: Copper Gains With US Tariffs Deadline Less Than a Month Away
articles.summary: Copper advanced in New York and London to kick off a crucial month that should deliver more clarity on the Trump administration’s plans for levies on imports of the metal to the US.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: For IndiGo, June quarter will be real earnings test of West Asia war impact
articles.summary: IndiGo’s shares had jumped 6% on the next trading day after CEO Walsh's appointment was announced on 31 March. To justify the steep valuation multiple that IndiGo is enjoying, it must achieve substantial improvement in profit hereon.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: IndiGo share price rises 5% despite a ₹2,536 crore loss in Q4 results. Should you buy or sell?
articles.summary: InterGlobe Aviation, the parent company of budget carrier IndiGo, share price rallied as much as 4.62% in Monday's trading session after the company posted its financial results for the quarter ending on March 31, 2026 on Friday.

articles.title: Gold price today: Rate drops on MCX amid stronger dollar, higher oil prices; experts highlight key levels to watch
articles.summary: Gold price today: Rate dropped on MCX amid a stronger dollar and higher oil prices. The dollar index rose by about 0.20%, making greenback-denominated gold expensive for buyers in other currencies. Crude oil prices jumped by over 2% amid persistent uncertainty over a US-Iran deal.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Rupee opens 3 paise higher at 94.97 against US dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened at 94.97 against the US dollar, slightly higher by 3 paise. Rising crude oil prices and foreign portfolio outflows are pressuring the currency, while recent RBI interventions have provided some recovery support amidst concerns over inflation and currency stability.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Raja Venkatraman, MarketSmith recommend five stocks for 1 June
articles.summary: Indian stocks fell sharply on May 29, erasing gains and dragging the Sensex down by over 1,000 points. As June begins, analysts suggest a flat start, with institutional flows and geopolitical factors influencing market trends.

articles.title: India’s manufacturing PMI climbs to 55.0 in May as output hits three-month high
articles.summary: Driven by domestic demand resilience, Indian factory activity outperformed estimates in May as new orders and production grew at their fastest pace since February despite persistent cost pressures.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: GST collections rise 3.2% to ₹1.94 trillion in May
articles.summary: Excluding the impact of a one-time ₹10,000-crore GST payment made by a telecom operator for spectrum allocation in May 2025, gross collections recorded an adjusted growth of 9% during May

articles.title: Powell says Fed will lose credibility if president removes officials over policy differences
articles.summary: The remarks were his first since stepping down as Federal Reserve chair.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt to help 150,000 farmers access machinery, curb stubble burning
articles.summary: The government aims to support 150,000 small farmers with ₹8,550 crore for farm equipment access, promoting mechanisation and reducing stubble burning through the establishment of custom hiring centres, enhancing productivity and resource efficiency in agriculture.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Rain, thunderstorm seen in many parts, conditions favour monsoon progress: IMD
articles.summary: The IMD has forecast heavy rain in parts of northeast and south India, along with severe thunderstorms, hailstorms and winds up to 90 kmph across several regions. As heatwave conditions ease, southwest monsoon is expected to advance further over Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Lakshadweep and adjoining seas.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Is 2026 heading for its driest monsoon since 2015?
articles.summary: IMD has lowered its monsoon outlook to 90% of normal, raising concerns over heatwaves, farm output, and food prices as El Niño risks loom large.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: How the stress is building in real estate globally, explained in five charts
articles.summary: After soaring in the wake of the covid pandemic, real estate prices globally have been cooling down. The trickle-down effects of the US-Iran war could prolong that.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Govt warns of fresh inflation risks: Fuel prices, monsoon to further squeeze your household budget
articles.summary: The finance ministry's report comes just days ahead of RBI’s Monetary Policy decision scheduled to be announced next week.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India eases fuel export levies, lowers duties on petrol, diesel and ATF starting June 1
articles.summary: The duty on exports of petrol has been set at ₹1.5 ($0.0158) per litre while that on diesel has been set at ₹13.5 per litre. Export duties on ATF have been set at ₹9.5 per litre.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Will AI put economists out of business? Fed’s John Williams says 'I’m not a believer that...'
articles.summary: The implications of artificial intelligence gripped global central bankers in Iceland this week, with one Federal Reserve official joking that it won’t put economists out of business.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: The week in charts: Households’ health bills, emerging markets tracker, childcare gap
articles.summary: In this weekly Plain Facts compilation, we present to you data-based insights, with easy-to-read charts, to help you delve deeper into the stories reported by Mint in the week gone by.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India opens CSR funding to non-profits via social stock exchange in landmark policy shift
articles.summary: India government has allowed companies to invest up to 10 per cent of their CSR funds in zero coupon zero principal instruments issued by not for profit organisations through a social stock exchange
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: RBI balance sheet jumps 20.6% on gold rally, rupee depreciation
articles.summary: Liquidity infusion through RBI’s open market purchases of government securities was also a factor for the expansion in the balance sheet
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: RBI says West Asia war clouds growth outlook, but India remains relatively resilient
articles.summary: RBI cautioned that a prolonged escalation of the war could hurt growth momentum and rekindle inflationary pressures through surging crude oil prices, volatile commodity markets and disruptions in shipping routes.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: India to get 'plastic notes'? RBI considers decade-old plan. What are polymer banknotes and are they better than paper?
articles.summary: Reserve Bank of India is reportedly reviving a decade-old plan to introduce polymer banknotes, with a pilot project expected soon. Here is what plastic currency is, why the RBI wants it and which countries already use it.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: Why India is delaying its key Q4 GDP data release by a week
articles.summary: The statistics ministry has shifted the release of Q4 GDP data from 31 May to 7 June each year. This year, the data will be released on 5 June, since 7 June is a Sunday.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: World’s rich pocket $1 billion with big sales of surging stocks
articles.summary: The transactions offer a snapshot into how wealthy private investors are locking in returns in larger positions as global stock indexes hit record highs despite geopolitical turmoil, fueled by enthusiasm for AI and rising corporate earnings.
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: India sees rising evening power shortages as solar supply fades
articles.summary: India's power grid faces challenges as evening demand surges after sunset, leading to widespread outages. A shortage of thermal power and reliance on spot markets contribute to the crisis, particularly in northern and western regions, with demand predicted to rise further due to agricultural needs.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Indonesia’s nickel woes due to West Asia war to hit India’s EV battery and stainless-steel sectors
articles.summary: India's electric vehicle and stainless-steel sectors face rising nickel prices and supply uncertainties due to the West Asia war. The country's heavy reliance on nickel imports, particularly from Indonesia, poses risks of increased costs and planning uncertainties for manufacturers and consumers.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why India wants to go back to standard edible oil pack sizes
articles.summary: The government plans to bring back standard pack sizes for edible oils on complaints that similar-looking pouches with different quantities and prices were making value-for-money comparisons difficult for buyers. The move aims to improve transparency and ease shopping choices.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: Karnataka Politics: Inside Siddaramaiah’s economic model — welfare push, record revenues and excise reforms
articles.summary: In the last three years, Siddaramaiah led Congress government has heavily prioritised a ‘pro-poor; Universal Basic Income model, funded by tax restructuring and robust state GDP growth, to balance economic expansion with social equity
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: How much ethanol can your car handle? You may get to choose soon at a fuel station
articles.summary: The government has advised state-run Indian Oil Corp. Ltd, Bharat Petroleum Corp. Ltd and Hindustan Petroleum Corp. Ltd, as well as privately owned Jio-bp Mobility, Nayara Energy and Shell to begin work on dispensing infrastructure for E20, E22, E25 and E30 fuels
articles.label: BULLISH

articles.title: From dirty dozen to IBC 2.0: how debt resolution changed in India
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code completes a decade having resolved over 1,400 large corporate cases and helping halve gross bad loans in the banking system. The April 2026 amendments now usher in IBC 2.0.
articles.label: BEARISH

articles.title: Ten years of recovery: How a bankruptcy legislation turned India's banks around
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code framework's biggest achievement has been shifting power from defaulting promoters to creditors, altering borrowing and lending behaviour across the system.
articles.label: NEUTRAL

articles.title: US trade delegation to visit India from 1-4 June for trade agreement talks as concerns remain over agri imports
articles.summary: A US official team will visit India from 1-4 June to discuss a bilateral trade agreement after prior negotiations. The interim agreement aims to enhance market access and cooperation across various sectors while addressing domestic sensitivities, particularly in agriculture.
articles.label: BULLISH
